summary: Clarusway is an online IT bootcamp that retrains career changers with no tech background into job-ready professionals in Cloud & DevOps, Data Science & AI, Full Stack Development, Salesforce, Cybersecurity, and more over 3-8 months of part-time study. Programs cost $7,000-$8,000 with flexible financing (ISA, installments, 30% upfront discount) and the company markets an outcome: graduates reportedly land tech jobs averaging $90K-$102K. Its funnel runs on free lead magnets (a 50+ hour mini bootcamp, a career-path quiz, info sessions) feeding advisor-assisted consultations that close enrollments.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Get a new free signup to their first hands-on win — running their first line of code — proving to a total beginner that tech is learnable for someone like them.
campaigns.trigger: Free Mini Bootcamp signup / account created (send instantly).
campaigns.segment: New contacts who signed up for the free Mini Bootcamp, the Career Path Quiz, or an info session; branch by the IT track they expressed interest in.
campaigns.rationale: Clarusway's whole conversion thesis hinges on the aha moment — a beginner successfully completing a hands-on exercise. Driving new free signups to that first win immediately is the single biggest predictor of whether they ever enroll or refer.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, kill the 'I'm not technical enough' fear, and drive to the one first action — running their first script inside the free Mini Bootcamp.
emails.subject: You're in — let's run your first line of code today
emails.preview: No IT background needed. Your free Mini Bootcamp (Python, Linux, Git, SQL) is unlocked.
keyPoints: Reassure directly: zero tech background required — thousands of former drivers, parents, and career changers started exactly here
One clear first action: log in and complete the first hands-on Python exercise (most beginners do it in under 15 minutes)
Set expectations for the next few short emails and offer a real human reply-to if they get stuck

campaigns.name: Lead-Magnet Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move warm-but-unconverted free leads (Mini Bootcamp users, quiz takers) toward booking a free advisor consultation by dismantling the #1 objection — 'I have no IT background.'
campaigns.trigger: Mini Bootcamp signup or Career Path Quiz completion; lead has not yet booked an advisor call.
campaigns.segment: Free-resource leads below the enrollment-ready threshold; branch the middle emails by the IT track from their quiz answer or Mini Bootcamp activity.
campaigns.rationale: The Mini Bootcamp and quiz generate a large pool of motivated leads who stall on self-doubt and cost. A proof-led nurture turning that fear into 'people like me did this' is Clarusway's highest-leverage free-to-enrolled conversion play.

emails.purpose: Evaluation-stage proof: a transformation story from a non-technical career changer that directly answers 'can someone like me really do this?' and drives to a free advisor call.
emails.subject: She drove for Uber. 8 months later: $96K cloud engineer.
emails.preview: No degree, no IT background — here's exactly how she made the switch.
keyPoints: Lead with a relatable grad: a complete career changer with zero tech background, told in her own words
Address the objection head-on — the part-time format and live mentor support are built for working beginners
Cite the reported ~91-92% job placement and $90K-$102K average starting salary with an honest 'results vary' disclaimer (FTC-safe)
Low-friction next step: a free 15-minute career consultation to map their personal path

campaigns.name: Consultation-to-Enrollment Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert prospects who took the free advisor call or started the 15-day risk-free trial but haven't enrolled — by neutralizing the cost and commitment objections.
campaigns.trigger: Free advisor consultation completed OR 15-day risk-free trial started; no application/payment submitted.
campaigns.segment: Post-consult non-converters and active trials; branch by stated track and by which objection surfaced on the call (cost vs. time vs. confidence).
campaigns.rationale: After the advisor call, the deal stalls on affordability and 'can I commit?' Clarusway's strongest answer is its financing menu plus the 15-day full refund — surfacing both at the moment of hesitation is the core revenue lever.

emails.purpose: Remove the cost barrier by laying out every payment option and the risk-free guarantee, then drive to the application.
emails.subject: Your tech career — and 5 ways to actually pay for it
emails.preview: ISA, 30% off upfront, or ~$250/mo. Plus 15 days risk-free to change your mind.
keyPoints: Reframe price as investment against the $90K-$102K outcome — the program pays for itself fast
Spell out the financing menu: ISA (pay 12.5% of income only after you earn $40K+, capped at $20K), 30% upfront discount, ~$250/mo installments, 0% education credit, Meritize loans
De-risk the decision: 15-day risk-free trial with a full refund — start, attend live classes, and only commit if it's right
Include required ISA disclosures and a 'results vary' placement disclaimer

campaigns.name: Booking, Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximize attendance at free advisor consultations and info sessions — the make-or-break revenue events in Clarusway's funnel.
campaigns.trigger: Advisor consultation or info session booked; upcoming appointment; no-show event.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled free consultation or info session registration.
campaigns.rationale: No-shows on the advisor call are the top pre-purchase churn signal for Clarusway. Reminders cut no-shows by ~35% on average, recovering enrollment-ready prospects who'd otherwise vanish before the close conversation.

emails.purpose: 24-hour reminder that confirms the call, builds anticipation for the value, and lowers no-show risk with an easy reschedule path.
emails.subject: Your free career call is tomorrow at {{time}}
emails.preview: 15 minutes to map your personal path into tech. Here's how to make it count.
keyPoints: Restate the payoff: a personalized track recommendation and a realistic plan to land a tech job — no pressure, no obligation
Tell them how to prep: bring their goals, current schedule, and any cost questions so the advisor can tailor it
One-click reschedule link to remove friction if their time changed (better a moved call than a no-show)
Reference the advisor by name and the specific track they're exploring

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate lapsed free leads — quiz takers and Mini Bootcamp users who never applied — and clean the list to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: 60-90 days of inactivity (no opens/clicks/logins) after a free-resource signup with no application.
campaigns.segment: Dormant free leads who took the quiz or started the Mini Bootcamp but never booked or enrolled.
campaigns.rationale: A large pool of motivated-then-stalled leads sits dormant after grabbing a free resource. A re-engagement nudge tied to the original dream — escaping a job they're stuck in — recovers 5-15% at near-zero cost and sunsets the rest to protect sender reputation.

emails.purpose: Reconnect to the core motivation, remind them the free resources and the outcome are still waiting, and offer a frictionless way back in.
emails.subject: Still want out of the job you're stuck in?
emails.preview: Your free Mini Bootcamp is still here — and so is the $90K+ tech career.
emails.cta: Pick up where you left off
keyPoints: Reconnect emotionally to why they signed up: the goal of a better-paying, more fulfilling tech career hasn't changed
Remind them the free Mini Bootcamp and Career Path Quiz are still unlocked — restarting takes minutes
Add a fresh reason to return: a recent grad win or a newly added track relevant to their original interest
Offer a low-pressure free advisor call as the alternative next step for the ready-but-hesitant
